_comment: Synthetic meeting recaps + transcript excerpts + action items for Northbridge Health Network. Two DISTINCT committees (Quality Steering, Credentialing) plus the Westgate EHR rollout program review. action_items carry their own ids, owners (people ids), due dates, status. Committee meetings are acl-scoped to internal staff personas (NOT the external vendor); the rollout review is visible to the vendor too. Administrative/non-clinical only — NO PHI. Reference 'today' is mid-June 2026.

meetings.title: Quality Steering Committee — Monthly Meeting
meetings.recap: Last Quality Steering Committee meeting (10-JUN). The committee DECIDED to adopt the revised medication-reconciliation policy, which requires a documented reconciliation at every transition of care (admission, transfer, discharge) across all network clinics, replacing the prior admission-only standard. The committee assigned the follow-up: Angela Foster owns finalising the policy document and David Munoz owns updating the operational workflow and training material. This is tracked as corrective action CAPA-001, due 09-JUN. The committee also reviewed the open environment-of-care documentation gap (CAPA-004) ahead of the Joint Commission readiness review and confirmed it remains open and owned by Priya Raman.
meetings.transcript_excerpt: Maria Delgado: 'We're standardising reconciliation at every transition of care, not just admission. Angela, you own the policy document; David, you own the workflow and training.' Angela Foster: 'Understood — I'll have the final draft for compliance review.' Priya Raman: 'Remember CAPA-004, the environment-of-care documentation, is still open and we need it for the readiness review.'

action_items.text: Finalise the revised medication-reconciliation policy document for compliance review
Update medication-reconciliation operational workflow and staff training materials
Compliance review of revised policy against Joint Commission medication-management standards

meetings.title: Quality Steering Committee — Monthly Meeting
meetings.recap: Prior Quality Steering Committee meeting (27-MAY). The committee opened two corrective actions ahead of the Joint Commission readiness review: CAPA-001 to revise the medication-reconciliation policy (owner Angela Foster) and CAPA-004 to close an environment-of-care documentation gap for the Westgate clinic (owner Priya Raman, due 06-JUN). Initial discussion of the medication-reconciliation scope began here but no decision was reached; the decision was deferred to the 10-JUN meeting.
meetings.transcript_excerpt: Maria Delgado: 'We have two corrective actions to land before the readiness review — the med-reconciliation policy and the environment-of-care documentation for Westgate.' Priya Raman: 'I'll own the environment-of-care item, target early June.'

action_items.text: Draft options for revised medication-reconciliation policy scope
Open environment-of-care documentation corrective action for Westgate clinic

meetings.title: Credentialing Committee — Monthly Meeting
meetings.recap: Last Credentialing Committee meeting (05-JUN). The committee CLOSED the provider-onboarding gap that the Quality Steering Committee had flagged as related to the readiness review: the primary-source verification backlog for newly onboarded providers was cleared and the onboarding checklist updated. This closure is tracked as corrective action CAPA-002 (now Closed). A separate HR-sensitive personnel matter (CAPA-005) was discussed in closed session and its detail is restricted to the credentialing committee and operations leadership; only the existence and closure-status of that item is shared more broadly.
meetings.transcript_excerpt: Sandra Okafor: 'The onboarding verification backlog is cleared — CAPA-002 is closed, and I've confirmed the updated checklist with operations.' James Whitaker: 'Good, that closes the gap Quality raised for the readiness review.' Sandra Okafor: 'The personnel matter we discussed stays in closed session.'

action_items.text: Confirm primary-source verification backlog cleared and update onboarding checklist
Notify Quality Steering Committee that the provider-onboarding gap (CAPA-002) is closed

meetings.title: Westgate EHR Rollout — Program Review
meetings.recap: Westgate EHR rollout program review (09-JUN). Status: the LuminaEHR build for the Westgate clinic is configured; end-user training is ~80% complete; the outstanding go-live blocker is validation of the medication-list data migration, owned by Greg Sullivan and due 13-JUN. The target go-live date is 22-JUN, pending Lumina's written confirmation that migration validation and training sign-off are complete. Robert Klein is to confirm the date with the vendor.
meetings.transcript_excerpt: Robert Klein: 'Training's at eighty percent. The one thing standing between us and go-live is the medication-list migration validation.' Greg Sullivan: 'I'll have the validation done by the thirteenth.' James Whitaker: 'Target is the twenty-second if Lumina confirms in writing.'

action_items.text: Complete and validate the Westgate medication-list data migration
Obtain written go-live date confirmation from Lumina Health Systems
